center.name: 保和省
center.summary: 保和省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括Black-faced coucal、Everett's scops owl、Philippine eagle-owl等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is endemic to the Philippines found in the islands of Mindanao, Basilan Dinagat Islands Bohol, Leyte, Biliran and Samar

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The most affected part of its range is Bohol which only has 4% forest cover remaining. Occurs in a few protected areas like Pasonanca Natural Park, Mount Apo and Mount Kitanglad on Mindanao, Rajah Sikatuna Protected Landscape in Bohol and Samar Island Natural Park but actual protection and enforcement of laws surrounding illegal logging and hunting are lacking

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The northern silvery kingfisher (Ceyx flumenicola) is a species of bird in the family Alcedinidae that is endemic to the Philippines being found in the Visayas on the islands of Bohol, Leyte and Samar

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is endemic to the Philippines, where it is found in lowland forests on the islands of Catanduanes, Samar, Bohol, Mindanao, Luzon, Leyte and possibly Sibuyan.Philippine Eagle-Owl

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is endemic to the Philippines being found in the islands of Luzon, Mindanao, Samar Leyte, and Bohol

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |name-list-style=amp |title=A possible new subspecies of the Philippine Hawk-eagle (Spizaetus philippensis) and its future prospects.}} It is endemic to the Philippines native to the islands of Leyte, Samar, Negros, Basilan, Bohol and Mindanao

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Indonesia, it occurs in Sumatra, Simeulue and Mentawai off the west side and Riau, Bangka Island and Belitung, Java, Borneo; and the western and southern Philippines on Palawan, Calamian Islands, Lubang Island, Mindoro, Mindanao and Bohol. The changeable hawk-eagle inhabits savannawoodland, agricultural land, wetlands, tea plantations, forest villages and even suburbs

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in forests on the islands of Samar, Calicoan, Leyte and Bohol in the east-central Philippines
